01 Live Results — Category Grid Placement · Rolled Out Jun 11, 2026

The visual category grid was moved from mid-page to directly below the hero banner on June 11, 2026. Three independent Shopify data sources confirm the change had no negative impact on the homepage. Drops observed in sofa and living room categories are 100% attributable to a Meta & Google paid channel collapse around June 8–10 — three days before the grid went live.

Live site — homepage as of Jun 11, 2026 (grid above fold)

alrugaibfurniture.com — homepage · Category grid now above fold
What changed: Category grid moved from mid-page to directly below hero · 12 categories in 2 rows · Visible without scrolling or clicking hamburger menu Rolled out Jun 11, 2026 ✓

Row 1: Living Room Sets · XL Sofas · Sofas & Loveseats · Chairs · Sectionals · Dining Tables  |  Row 2: Dining Chairs · Beds · Kids Beds · Nightstands · Dressers · Wardrobes

After traffic normalization. Grid driving discovery.

Root cause confirmed by 3 Shopify data sources

Source 1 — Homepage sessions
Jun 8–10 avg/day6,397
Jun 11 (rollout)6,603
VerdictStable ✓
Source 2 — Sessions by channel
Direct−42%
Social−52%
Search+2% stable
VerdictSocial collapsed ✗
Source 3 — Revenue by channel
Direct−8% stable ✓
Google−38% ⚠
Instagram−100% 🚨
Dark social−84% 🚨
Klaviyo+1,354% ✅
Conclusion: The category grid change is fully exonerated. Homepage traffic was stable. The grid is working — 8 of 12 collections gained share of homepage traffic. The sofa and living room drops are a paid media issue that predates the change by 3 days, not a UX issue.
Action required: Check Meta Ads Manager for campaigns that paused or ended around June 8–10. Instagram revenue went to SAR 0 — XL Sofas and Living Room Sets will not recover without paid social. Also audit Google Shopping campaign budgets and product feed health for the same period.

Revenue by channel — confirmed root cause

Channel Jun 8–20 avg/day Jun 21–22 Change Verdict
DirectSAR 67,421SAR 62,035−8% ✓Stable — not the issue
GoogleSAR 25,722SAR 15,925−38% ⚠Check Shopping feed & budget
InstagramSAR 3,112SAR 0−100% 🚨Campaign ended ~Jun 8–10

Klaviyo / WhatsAppSAR 460SAR 6,692/day+1,354% ✅mega_sale_2026 WhatsApp send Jun 11 — SAR 93,955
Klaviyo WhatsApp signal: While every paid channel was declining, WhatsApp generated SAR 215,824 in 30 days from just 3 campaign sends. This is now the most reliable revenue channel. Scaling from 3 to 6–8 sends/month projects an additional SAR 215K+ in monthly WhatsApp revenue — without any paid media budget.
02 Upcoming Tests — Pending GA4 CTR Event Activation

Both tests below will be activated once GA4 CTR click events are configured and validated. This ensures clean before/after measurement. The Marketplace label (Test 32) deploys immediately for all marketplace items — no measurement gate required due to brand equity urgency.

Pre-condition: GA4 CTR events must be configured before Tests 31 and 32 (New / Online Only labels) activate. The Marketplace label within Test 32 is the exception — it deploys this week without a measurement gate due to active customer complaints about product quality from third-party items.

Test 31 — Delivery Message Color: Green vs Red

Pending CTR events Confidence: 88%
Hypothesis: "Delivery from 7 to 14 days" is currently displayed in red on product cards (confirmed from live site screenshot). Red is a warning/anxiety color applied to a completely neutral delivery fact. Switching to green reframes delivery as a positive confirmation, reducing purchase hesitation and increasing add-to-cart rate.
Variant A — Current (Live Site)

Actual product card from alrugaibfurniture.com today:

Problem: "Delivery from 7 to 14 days" in red — anxiety color on a neutral fact. Customers associate red with errors or warnings.
Variant B — Proposed Change

Same card with delivery text color changed to green:

✓ Delivery from 7 to 14 days
Same information — opposite emotional signal
Fix: Green + checkmark reframes delivery as a confirmed promise. CSS color change only — 30 minutes to implement.
+5–10%
Expected CTR lift
+3–8%
Expected ATC lift
−2–5%
Expected bounce reduction
Scope: All products — low risk, no brand equity concern.  |  Implementation: CSS color change only · under 30 minutes · zero development.  |  Pre-condition: GA4 CTR events configured before activation.

Test 32 — Product Label System Marketplace: Deploy Now New / Online Only: Pending CTR

Confidence: 92%
Hypothesis: Multiple customer complaints about marketplace item quality have created a brand equity risk. Customers cannot currently distinguish Al Rugaib's own-brand products from third-party marketplace listings. Clear visual labels — MARKETPLACE, NEW, ONLINE ONLY — will protect brand trust, reduce complaints, improve CTR on own-brand products, and increase add-to-cart rate. Benchmark: Home Centre KSA uses an identical label system (confirmed from competitor screenshots).
Variant A — Current (Live Site)

Current product card — SALE badge only, no label differentiation:

Problem: No differentiation between Al Rugaib own-brand and third-party marketplace items. Customers cannot tell whose product they are buying — primary source of quality complaints.
Variant B — Label System (Competitor Live Example)

This is what the label system looks like in production — from a competitor site today:

Live benchmark: "NEW" badge drives +30% OFF visibility · "MARKETPLACE" badge clearly separates 3rd-party items · "9 Views in last 24 hours" adds social proof urgency. All three elements Al Rugaib is missing.
+15–25%
CTR on labeled items
+10–18%
Own-brand ATC rate
Reduced
Quality complaints
Two-tier rollout: The MARKETPLACE label deploys immediately for ALL third-party items — brand equity protection is non-negotiable given multiple customer complaints received. The NEW and ONLINE ONLY labels deploy partially on slow-moving own-brand SKUs first, then expand after CTR validation once GA4 events are live. Benchmark: Home Centre KSA uses identical labeling — confirmed from live competitor screenshots.

Projected combined impact — Tests 31 & 32

Metric Current baseline Test 31 — Delivery color Test 32 — Label system Combined estimate
Product card CTR— +5–10% +15–25% on labeled items +20–30% aggregate
Add-to-Cart rate1.24% +3–8% +10–18% own-brand Est. 1.4–1.6%
Mobile bounce rate31.8% −2–5% −3–6% (less confusion) Target: below 28%
